sources.title: FDA's Concerns with Unapproved GLP-1 Drugs Used for Weight Loss

claims.statement: DailyMed identifies Ozempic as semaglutide injection indicated as an adjunct to diet and exercise to improve glycemic control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus and for certain cardiovascular-risk contexts in adults with type 2 diabetes.

extractedEvidence.quote: OZEMPIC is a glucagon-like peptide 1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist indicated...

reviewFlags: product-specific indication; do not generalize to compounded products

claims.statement: DailyMed identifies Wegovy as semaglutide indicated with diet and physical activity for chronic weight-management contexts and cardiovascular-risk reduction contexts described in the product label.

context.population: label-specified obesity, overweight, or cardiovascular-risk populations

extractedEvidence.quote: WEGOVY is a glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist indicated in combination with a reduced calorie diet and increased physical activity

reviewFlags: product-specific indication; verify latest label before publication

claims.statement: DailyMed semaglutide product labels list personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN 2 as a contraindication.

context.population: patients with personal or family history of MTC or MEN 2

extractedEvidence.quote: Personal or family history of MTC or in patients with MEN 2

claims.statement: FDA has warned about dosing errors and adverse events associated with compounded injectable semaglutide products; BioStack should not treat compounded-product claims as equivalent to approved product labels.

context.population: users of compounded injectable semaglutide products

extractedEvidence.quote: FDA received multiple reports of adverse events, some requiring hospitalization, that may be related to dosing errors associated with compounded injectable semaglutide products.
extractedEvidence.pageOrSection: FDA concerns with unapproved GLP-1 drugs

reviewFlags: compounded-product claims require separate evidence and review

reviewReasons: Pilot regulatory evidence packet requires human review before publication.
